title: sarcocapnos enneaphylla var. saetabensis
intro: Sarcocapnos enneaphylla var. saetabensis, also known as the Sarcocapnos of Saetabensia, is a type of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to the European Alps. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a source of sanguinarine, a toxic alkaloid with potential medicinal uses. The health benefits of Sarcocapnos enneaphylla var. saetabensis include its possible anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made through drying and powdering the plant material, which can then be used in teas, tinctures, or other forms of supplements.
